text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Consumer spending and labor market show weakness, suggesting economic headwinds. This could pressure growth-oriented tech names, especially with high valuations.

Your holdings: SOXL (-21.99%) and SPAL (-23.14%) are leveraged, decaying, and have breached their stops, needing immediate attention. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny, illiquid spec. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on further signs of slowing consumer demand and labor market weakness. Monitor your leveraged ETFs closely.

Bottom line: Stay sharp on risk management, especially with leveraged positions, as economic signals are mixed and drawdowns hurt extra on margin.
plays: Trim SOXL: It's below its 200 stop at 198.53. This leveraged ETF is a SELL and decaying.
Trim SPAL: It's below its 21.38 stop at 19.33. This new, leveraged ETF is volatile and decaying.
No new buys today - hold and watch.
